title: NFL executives reportedly ‘expect’ Patriots to acquire A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: NFL executives anticipate the Patriots will acquire Eagles WR A.J. Brown after June 1 due to salary cap considerations.
Philadelphia seeks a first-round draft pick, possibly more, in exchange for A.J. Brown, a three-time Pro Bowler.
Patriots' interest in Brown remains discreet, with focus on strengthening the roster via various acquisition strategies.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: '28 Years Later: The Bone Temple,' directed by Nia DaCosta, is now streaming on Netflix.
'The Testament of Ann Lee,' featuring Amanda Seyfried, is available for streaming on Hulu.
Season 2 of 'Your Friends and Neighbors' with Jon Hamm streams on Apple TV.

title: Caleb Durbin’s redemption, Willson Contreras’s swagger & other takeaways from Red Sox’ home-opening win
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Caleb Durbin broke an 0-for-34 slump with an RBI single, redeeming after an earlier double play.
Willson Contreras hit his first Red Sox home run, contributing to a three-run sixth inning at Fenway.
Sonny Gray delivered a strong start, allowing two runs over six innings, helping Red Sox defeat Padres 5-2.

title: Here are the dates for the Patriots’ offseason workout schedule
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The New England Patriots begin their offseason workout program on April 20, starting with conditioning drills and meetings.
Phase two, with on-field drills but no contact, runs for three weeks starting after the initial conditioning phase.
Mandatory minicamp takes place June 15-17 following several OTAs, concluding the offseason program before July training camp.

title: Patriots double up on edge rushers in latest ESPN mock draft
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: ESPN's latest mock draft projects the Patriots to select edge rusher Cashius Howell at No. 31 overall.
The mock draft predicts Gabe Jacas as a second-round pick for the Patriots, strengthening their pass rush.
Patriots coach Mike Vrabel emphasizes drafting best available players over focusing solely on team needs.

title: For a struggling Red Sox team, Friday’s opener at Fenway Park provided a fresh start
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Red Sox secured a 5-2 win over Padres in Fenway home opener, halting a previous losing streak.
Pitcher Sonny Gray delivered six strong innings, while Willson Contreras hit a decisive 423-foot home run.
Marcelo Mayer and Caleb Durbin achieved key performances, contributing to the team's improved dynamics.

title: North Andover police working to get body cameras after Kelsey Fitzsimmons shooting
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: North Andover is pursuing a body-worn camera program for police, nine months after an officer shooting incident.
Officer Kelsey Fitzsimmons was acquitted of assault charges after she was shot by a colleague in her home.
Massachusetts has provided nearly $13 million in grants for body camera programs to at least 144 local departments.

title: Xander Bogaerts talks Roman Anthony, pressure from fans in his return to Fenway Park
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Xander Bogaerts returned to Fenway Park with the Padres, reflecting on changes since leaving the Red Sox in 2022.
Bogaerts discussed the heightened expectations Boston fans have compared to San Diego, highlighting the pressure and winning culture.
Roman Anthony, a young Red Sox outfielder, is seen by Bogaerts as a potential new face of the franchise.

title: Step into Boston’s past this season
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Boston's Freedom Trail, a 2.5-mile path connecting 16 historical sites, attracts over 4 million visitors annually.
Six Flags New England debuts Quantum Accelerator, a dual-launch straddle coaster, opening for all guests on April 17.
Restaurant Week Portsmouth & the Seacoast offers prix fixe menus from April 9-18, with meals priced at $32, $42, and $52.

title: Singer Vanessa Carlton’s noise complaint against R.I. neighbor ends up before state Supreme Court
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Vanessa Carlton's noise complaint against neighbors reached the Rhode Island Supreme Court after a preliminary injunction in 2024.
Defendants Avagyan and Beausoleil appealed the injunction, which limited noise and alleged harassment from their industrial property.
The Supreme Court has yet to rule on the appeal; Carlton alleges continuous noise violates local decibel limits.
